details: Same package (qr-code-styling-temp@9.9.10) as LWA-2026-4820, confirmed malware. Install hook loads obfuscated DNS beacon that collects username, hostname, and cwd, then exfiltrates them via dns.resolve4 queries to oob[.]sl4x0[.]xyz — an OOB DNS exfiltration channel during npm install. Combosquats the qr-code-styling package. Version 9.9.10 is version-gaming to appear established. The install hook (node index.js) silently loads lib/core.js, which imports an obfuscated DNS beacon (lib/6ad264.js and lib/b02e30.js). At runtime during install, it collects the victim's username (os.userInfo().username), hostname (os.hostname()), and cwd basename, then encodes them into a DNS lookup to <subdomain>.oob[.]sl4x0[.]xyz via dns.resolve4. This is an OOB data exfiltration channel — the attacker's DNS server logs the host info embedded in the query domain. The src/ files are well-written decoy utility code unrelated to QR code styling. No token theft observed but the DNS beacon is a clear host-fingerprinting exfil on install.
